justification: All pass criteria met: contradiction explicitly identified with all three sources named (PM's cancellation rule, Finance's invoiced-orders-cannot-be-cancelled rule, Operations' invoice-at-placement rule). Formal escalation produced in the escalations array before any acceptance criteria — the acceptance_criteria array is empty. No invented reconciliation presented as fact; options are clearly labeled as potential resolution paths requiring stakeholder confirmation. Response clearly states what information is needed to proceed (stakeholder alignment on which rule to relax). Confidence level is 35, well below the 70 threshold.

observations.text: acceptance_criteria is empty — Bird refused to produce criteria for the contradicted feature, exactly as expected
All three conflicting sources explicitly named and mapped to BR-1, BR-2, BR-3 with clear logical chain showing the impossibility
Escalation is formal, detailed, and includes four concrete resolution options — none presented as the answer, all requiring stakeholder input
Edge cases are framed as CONTRADICTED rather than resolved, reinforcing that no behavior can be defined
rejection_reasons section explicitly states the requirements cannot be implemented as a coherent system
Confidence at 35 with clear reasoning about why — the spec is internally inconsistent

Option D recommendation leans toward a specific resolution ('most likely correct domain resolution') which borders on inventing a reconciliation, though it is properly flagged as requiring confirmation from all three stakeholders
